name: Qarabağ FK
content:
Qarabağ FK is one of the most successful and recognizable football clubs in Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. Founded in 1987 (with roots dating back to 1950), the club originally represents the city of Aghdam but has been based in Baku since 1993 due to the Nagorno-Karabakh conflict. Despite displacement, Qarabağ has remained a symbol of resilience and national identity in Azerbaijani football.

Domestically, Qarabağ has been highly dominant, winning multiple league titles and national cups, including becoming one of the most decorated clubs in the country. They were also the first non-Baku club to win the national championship after independence and have consistently competed at the top level since the early 1990s. Their modern success is largely associated with long-time manager Gurban Gurbanov, under whom the club has achieved sustained dominance and stability.

Internationally, Qarabağ has elevated Azerbaijani football’s profile by regularly participating in UEFA competitions. The club became the first from Azerbaijan to reach the group stage of the UEFA Champions League in the 2017–18 season and has also recorded multiple appearances in the Europa League and Conference League. These achievements, along with strong performances against major European teams, have established Qarabağ as a consistent and competitive presence beyond domestic football.

name: Neftçi PFK
content:
Neftçi PFK is one of the oldest and most successful football clubs in Azerbaijan, based in Baku and founded in 1937. Originally known as Neftyanik, the club became a prominent representative of Azerbaijani football during the Soviet era, competing in the top division of the USSR championship and achieving its best result in 1966 by finishing third.

Following Azerbaijan’s independence in 1991, Neftçi established itself as a dominant force in domestic football, winning multiple league titles and national cups. The club is widely regarded as one of the country’s most historic and popular teams, often competing at the top level and playing a key role in shaping Azerbaijani football culture.

In international competitions, Neftçi has represented Azerbaijan regularly in UEFA tournaments. A major milestone came in the 2012–13 season when the club became the first Azerbaijani team to reach the group stage of a European competition (UEFA Europa League). These achievements have helped raise the profile of Azerbaijani clubs on the European stage.

name: Kapaz PFK
content:
Kapaz PFK is a professional football club based in Ganja, Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. Founded in 1959 (originally under different names such as Toxucu and Dinamo Kirovabad), the club has a long history that dates back to the Soviet era, when it even reached the Soviet Top League. Today, Kapaz remains one of the most traditional clubs outside the capital Baku.

Following Azerbaijan’s independence, Kapaz enjoyed its most successful period in the 1990s, winning the national championship three times and the Azerbaijan Cup four times. One of its most notable achievements came in the 1997–98 season, when the club won the league undefeated—an exceptional record in Azerbaijani football. These successes established Kapaz as one of the country’s historically significant teams.

In the 2000s, however, the club faced serious financial difficulties, leading to relegation and even temporary exclusion from the top division. Despite these setbacks, Kapaz managed to recover, returning to the Premier League and maintaining a strong and loyal fanbase, often among the largest in Azerbaijan. Today, the club continues to compete at the top level, representing the city of Ganja and preserving its legacy in Azerbaijani football.

name: Şamaxı FK
content:
Şamaxı FK is a professional football club based in Shamakhi, Azerbaijan, currently competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. Founded in 1997, the club has undergone several name and identity changes throughout its history, reflecting organizational and structural transformations within Azerbaijani football.

Originally established as Khazar University FC, the team later became known as Inter Baku, during which it achieved its greatest successes, including winning the national championship in the 2007–08 season and again in 2009. During this period, the club also competed in European competitions such as the UEFA Champions League and Europa League, marking an important stage in its development.

In later years, the club was renamed Keşlə FK (2017–2022) before adopting its current identity as Şamaxı FK. Despite frequent rebranding and relocation, it remains a stable participant in Azerbaijani top-flight football, with two league titles in its history and continued involvement in domestic and European competitions.

name: Shamkir FC
content:
Shamkir FC was a professional football club based in the city of Şəmkir, Azerbaijan. Founded in 1954 and later re-established in 1993 after the collapse of the Soviet Union, the club became one of the notable teams in Azerbaijani football, although it never competed in the Soviet top leagues.

The club’s most successful period came in the late 1990s and early 2000s, when it won multiple Azerbaijan Premier League titles, including back-to-back championships in 1999–2000 and 2000–01, and also reached several Azerbaijan Cup finals. During this era, Shamkir was among the strongest teams in the country and even became the first Azerbaijani club to progress through a qualifying round in the UEFA Champions League.

Despite its success, the club faced recurring financial difficulties, leading to temporary dissolutions and re-formations, particularly in 2005 and later in 2009. After competing mostly in lower divisions in its later years, Shamkir FC was ultimately dissolved in 2017, marking the end of one of Azerbaijan’s historically significant football clubs.
stadium: Shamkir Olympic Sport Complex Stadium, Cap. 2,000

name: FC Baku
content:
FC Baku was a professional football club based in Baku, Azerbaijan, founded in 1997 as Dinamo Baku. The club quickly established itself in the domestic league, finishing as runners-up in its debut top-flight season and later becoming a regular participant in the Azerbaijan Premier League. Over time, it developed a reputation as one of the stronger teams in the country.

The most successful period in FC Baku’s history came in the mid-to-late 2000s, when the club won two league titles (2005–06 and 2008–09) and three Azerbaijan Cups. During these years, it also competed in UEFA competitions, contributing to the growing international presence of Azerbaijani football. The club’s identity, colors, and branding were closely tied to the city of Baku, and it attracted a solid local fanbase.

However, financial difficulties began to affect the club in the 2010s, leading to a decline in performance and status. FC Baku became an amateur team in 2016 and ultimately ceased operations in 2018. Despite its relatively short existence, the club remains an important part of modern Azerbaijani football history due to its domestic success and role in European competitions.

name: Khazar Lankaran FK
content:
Khazar Lankaran FK was a professional football club based in Lankaran, Azerbaijan, founded in 2004 by businessman Mübariz Mənsimov. The club quickly rose to prominence thanks to strong financial backing, earning the nickname “Caucasian Chelsea.” It played its home matches at Lankaran City Stadium and became one of the most popular teams outside the capital, building a passionate fanbase.

Khazar Lankaran enjoyed its most successful period in the mid-2000s, winning the Azerbaijan Premier League title in the 2006–07 season and securing multiple Azerbaijan Cups. The club also achieved international success by winning the CIS Cup in 2008 and regularly participating in UEFA competitions, helping raise the profile of Azerbaijani football abroad.

Despite early success, the club faced financial and administrative problems in the 2010s, leading to declining performances. After finishing near the bottom of the league and being denied a license for the 2016–17 season, Khazar Lankaran withdrew from professional football and was effectively dissolved in 2016, ending the short but impactful history of one of Azerbaijan’s notable clubs.

name: Turan Tovuz
content:
Turan Tovuz is a professional football club based in the city of Tovuz, Azerbaijan, and currently competes in the Azerbaijan Premier League. Founded in 1992, it is considered the first professional football club in independent Azerbaijan and has played an important role in the early development of the national league system.

The club enjoyed its greatest success in the early 1990s, finishing among the top teams in its first seasons and winning the national championship in 1993–94. Turan Tovuz also became one of the regular participants in the top division, remaining there for over two decades. However, from the late 1990s onward, financial difficulties led to a gradual decline in performance and league standings.

In the 2012–13 season, the club was relegated from the top flight for the first time in its history, ending a long run in the highest division. After several years in the lower leagues, including a First Division title, Turan Tovuz eventually returned to the Premier League in 2022. Today, the club remains a historic representative of regional Azerbaijani football and continues to compete at the top level.

name: Zira FK
content:
Zira FK is a professional football club based in Zirə (a settlement in Baku), Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was founded in 2014 and quickly rose through the domestic system, joining professional competitions immediately after its creation. It plays its home matches at the Zira Olympic Sport Complex Stadium and is nicknamed “The Eagles.”

Despite being a relatively young club, Zira FK has established itself as a competitive Premier League side. In its early seasons, the club achieved promotion to the top division and has since remained a stable participant in the league, often finishing in the upper half of the table. It has also built a reputation for strong organization and steady squad development rather than relying on long history or large financial backing.

In recent years, Zira has also become a regular participant in UEFA competitions, including the Europa League and Europa Conference League qualifying rounds. While not yet achieving major breakthroughs in Europe, the club has gained valuable international experience and continues to improve its domestic standing under coach Rashad Sadygov.
stadium: Zira Olympic Sport Complex Stadium, Cap. 1,500

name: Qabala İK
content:
Qəbələ İK (often known internationally as Gabala FK) is a professional football club based in the city of Qabala, Azerbaijan. Founded in 2005, the club quickly became part of the country’s top football structure and competes in the Azerbaijan Premier League. It represents one of the newer but more professionally structured clubs in Azerbaijani football.

The club has been one of the most stable teams outside Baku in the modern era, regularly finishing in the upper half of the league table. Gabala has also achieved notable domestic success by finishing runners-up in the Premier League multiple times and winning the Azerbaijan Cup in 2019 and 2023. These achievements helped establish it as a competitive force despite its relatively short history.

Internationally, Qəbələ has represented Azerbaijan in UEFA competitions, including the Europa League, where it reached the group stage—one of the few Azerbaijani clubs to do so. The club plays its home matches at Gabala City Stadium and is known for its modern infrastructure and focus on long-term development.

name: FK Khazar Sumgayit
content:
FK Khazar Sumgayit was a now-defunct Azerbaijani football club based in the city of Sumgait. The club was founded in 1960 under the name Metallurg and went through numerous name changes throughout its history, reflecting different eras and reorganizations within Soviet and later Azerbaijani football. It competed in domestic leagues during both the Soviet period and the early years of Azerbaijan’s independence.

After independence, the club became a frequent participant in the Azerbaijan Premier League and had several solid seasons, including finishing as runner-up in the early 1990s. However, its performances fluctuated significantly over time, and it struggled with consistency in the top division. Despite this, it remained an established part of Azerbaijani football throughout the 1990s and early 2000s.

Due to financial difficulties, FK Khazar Sumgayit was ultimately dissolved in 2004. Over its long history, the club underwent multiple identity changes and is remembered as one of the early post-Soviet teams that helped shape competitive football in Azerbaijan during the transition period.

name: Karvan İK
content:
Karvan İK is a professional football club based in Yevlakh, Azerbaijan, currently competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was originally founded in 1990 as FC Avtomobilchi, later re-established in 2004 as Karvan FK, and again restructured in 2022 under its current name. It plays its home matches at Yevlakh City Stadium.

Karvan experienced its strongest period in the mid-2000s shortly after entering the top division. In the 2004–05 and 2005–06 seasons, the club finished near the top of the league, including a runner-up finish, and also reached the Azerbaijan Cup final. During this time, Karvan also participated in European competitions such as the UEFA Cup and Intertoto Cup, marking its peak at domestic and continental level.

After 2007, the club began to struggle financially, leading to the departure of key players and a decline in performance. Karvan was eventually relegated in the 2009–10 season and later faced periods of dissolution and reformation due to financial instability. Despite these setbacks, the club has been revived and continues to compete in Azerbaijani football, aiming to re-establish itself in the top tier.

name: Khazri Buzovna
content:
Khazri Buzovna was a professional football club based in Buzovna, Azerbaijan, that competed in the Azerbaijan Premier League during the mid-1990s. The club was founded in 1992 and quickly entered the national league system following the country’s independence. It played its home matches in Buzovna, a settlement in the Baku area.

During its short existence, Khazri Buzovna had several competitive seasons in the top division. The club achieved its best results in the mid-1990s, including a strong league finish (runner-up level performance is often noted in records) and participation in the Azerbaijan Cup, where it also reached advanced stages. It also represented Azerbaijan in European competition, playing in the UEFA Cup qualifiers in the 1996–97 season against Hutnik Kraków.

However, financial problems affected the club toward the end of the decade. In the 1997–98 season, Khazri Buzovna withdrew from the league after only a few rounds and was subsequently dissolved in 1998, ending its brief but notable presence in early Azerbaijani professional football.

name: Sabah FK
content:
Sabah FK is a professional football club based in Masazır (near Baku), Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was founded relatively recently, on 8 September 2017, and quickly entered the national football system, becoming one of the newer projects in Azerbaijani top-flight football.

Despite its short history, Sabah has developed into a competitive Premier League side. After joining the top division in 2018, the club established itself as a stable mid-to-upper table team and has also finished near the top positions in recent seasons. It is known for structured management, modern facilities, and steady squad development rather than long tradition.

In recent years, Sabah FK has also started to build its presence in domestic cup competitions and European tournaments. The club has participated in UEFA qualifying rounds, gaining valuable international experience while continuing to grow its reputation within Azerbaijani football as an ambitious and rapidly developing team.

name: Shuvalan FK
content:
Shuvalan FK was a professional football club based in the Şüvəlan settlement of Baku, Azerbaijan. The club was founded in 1996 as a futsal team under the name AMMK and later transitioned into professional football in the mid-2000s. Over its history, it underwent several name changes, including Olimpik Baku, AZAL, and finally Shuvalan FK.

During its time in the Azerbaijan Premier League, the club had a mixed but sometimes competitive presence. Its best period came in the late 2000s, when it finished 2nd in the 2007–08 season and also had solid mid-table performances in other campaigns. Shuvalan also participated in UEFA competitions such as the UEFA Cup and Europa League qualifiers, representing Azerbaijan internationally.

In 2017, the club was officially renamed Shuvalan FK, but financial and organizational issues continued. Eventually, the team was dissolved in 2019, ending its professional existence after more than two decades of multiple restructurings and identity changes.

name: Araz-Naxçıvan PFK
content:
Araz-Naxçıvan PFK is a professional football club based in Nakhchivan, Azerbaijan, currently competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was originally founded in 1967 and has experienced multiple dissolutions and re-establishments due to financial and organizational issues, making its history one of the most turbulent among Azerbaijani clubs.

The team has been active in several different periods, including participation in the Soviet league system, a brief return in the early 2000s, and a major re-establishment in 2013. After winning the Azerbaijan First Division in 2013–14, the club earned promotion to the Premier League, but withdrew during the 2014–15 season due to disputes and financial instability. It was later revived again and returned to professional competition in the 2020s.

In recent years, Araz-Naxçıvan has stabilized and re-entered the top tier of Azerbaijani football, again competing in the Premier League after winning promotion from the First Division in 2023. The club is known for its resilience and repeated comebacks despite financial and administrative challenges throughout its history.

name: Sabail FK
content:
Sabail FK is a professional football club based in Baku, Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was founded in 2016 and is one of the newest teams in the country’s top division, quickly entering the professional system and establishing itself as part of the modern Azerbaijani football structure.

In its early years, Sabail progressed rapidly from the First Division to the Premier League and managed to secure a stable position in the top tier. The club has generally finished in mid-table positions, with occasional stronger seasons and participation in domestic cup competitions, showing gradual development rather than immediate dominance.

Sabail has also gained experience in European competitions, including qualification rounds of the UEFA Europa League, which marked an important milestone for such a young club. Although it has not yet achieved major trophies, it is considered a developing side focused on building consistency and long-term competitiveness in Azerbaijani football.

name: Simurq PIK
content:
Simurq PIK was a professional football club based in Zaqatala, Azerbaijan, that competed in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was founded in 2005 and quickly rose through the domestic system, earning promotion to the top division shortly after its creation. It was nicknamed “The Phoenixes” and played its home matches at Zaqatala City Stadium.

In its peak years, Simurq established itself as a competitive mid-table Premier League team. The club’s best league performance came in the 2008–09 season, when it finished 3rd in the Azerbaijan Premier League, which is considered its greatest achievement. It also had solid campaigns in other seasons, including a 7th-place finish and consistent top-flight participation.

Simurq made its European debut in the UEFA Europa League qualifiers but was eliminated in the early rounds. Financial and structural difficulties eventually led to the club’s dissolution in 2015, ending its short but notable presence in Azerbaijani football.

name: Sumgayit FK
content:
Sumgayit FK is a professional football club based in Sumgait, Azerbaijan, competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was founded in 2010 and is known as “The Chemists,” reflecting the city’s strong industrial and chemical industry background. It is one of the newer clubs in Azerbaijani top-flight football.

The team was quickly integrated into the professional system and gained promotion to the Premier League in 2011. Since then, Sumgayit has mostly focused on developing young domestic players, often fielding squads made up primarily of Azerbaijani talent. This youth-oriented strategy has become a key feature of the club’s identity.

In terms of performance, Sumgayit has generally been a mid-table side, occasionally achieving higher finishes such as a 4th-place result in recent seasons. The club has also participated in European competitions, including UEFA Europa League and Conference League qualifiers, gaining valuable international experience despite limited success in advancing deep into tournaments.

name: FK Viləş Masallı
content:
FK Viləş Masallı is a football club based in Masallı, Azerbaijan. The club was originally founded in 1967 as FK Masallı and has gone through several name changes, periods of inactivity, and re-establishments over its history. It is one of the regional clubs representing southern Azerbaijan football.

The club has competed in the Azerbaijan Premier League in different periods, most notably around the early 2000s and again during the late 2000s. Its strongest performances came in the 2000–01 season, when it finished in the top positions of the league and also participated in European competitions such as the UEFA Intertoto Cup.

However, financial instability repeatedly affected the club. FK Viləş Masallı was dissolved in 2008 after withdrawing from the top division, but later reformed in 2013 and returned to lower-level competitions, continuing to represent Masallı in Azerbaijani football in amateur and regional leagues.

name: İnşaatçı Baku FK
content:
İnşaatçı Baku FK was a professional football club based in Baku, Azerbaijan. The club was originally founded in 1935 as Stroitel Baku and later renamed İnşaatçı Baku in 1992 after Azerbaijan gained independence. It competed in the Azerbaijan Top Division during the early years of the national league system.

In the early 1990s, İnşaatçı Baku was part of the inaugural seasons of the Azerbaijan Premier League. One of its most notable achievements was winning the Azerbaijan Cup in 1992, making it the first club to win the competition in the independent era of Azerbaijani football.

The club played in the top division until the 1994–95 season, when it finished 11th and was subsequently dissolved in 1995 due to financial and organizational difficulties. Despite its short post-independence existence, İnşaatçı Baku remains historically significant as one of the founding participants of modern Azerbaijani football.

name: Shafa Baku FK
content:
Shafa Baku FK was a professional football club based in Baku, Azerbaijan. It was founded in 1998 and quickly joined the Azerbaijan Premier League, becoming part of the top division in its debut period. The club played its home matches at Shafa Stadium and was active mainly in the early 2000s.

One of the club’s biggest achievements came in the 2000–01 season, when Shafa Baku won the Azerbaijan Cup by defeating Neftçi Baku 2–1 in the final. During this era, the club also had strong league performances, including a 4th-place finish in the 2000–01 season and a runner-up position in 2001–02 before falling into the lower part of the table later.

Shafa also participated in European competition, playing in the UEFA Cup (now Europa League) in the 2001–02 season, although they were eliminated in the preliminary rounds. Financial problems eventually led to serious instability, and the club withdrew from the Premier League during the 2004–05 season, being effectively dissolved around 2005–2006.

name: Energetik FK
content:
Energetik FK is an Azerbaijani football club based in Mingachevir, and its history is closely tied to several earlier club identities and name changes. The club’s origins go back to 1966, when it was established and competed in Soviet-era football under different names such as Textilshchik, Avtomobilist, Kur, and later Kur-Nur Mingachevir. It has long been one of the traditional clubs from the region.

After Azerbaijan’s independence, the club continued under various forms and eventually re-emerged in the 2000s as Energetik Mingachevir FK. It has mostly competed in the lower tiers of Azerbaijani football, though it has occasionally reached the top division. One of its notable moments was promotion to the Premier League in the mid-2000s, although financial problems prevented it from sustaining that level.

Throughout its history, Energetik has been heavily affected by financial instability, leading to multiple restructurings, withdrawals, and re-establishments. Despite these challenges, the club remains active in the Azerbaijani football system, usually competing in the First Division and representing Mingachevir as one of its key football institutions.

name: İnşaatçı Sabirabad FK
content:
İnşaatçı Sabirabad FK was a football club based in Sabirabad, Azerbaijan. It was founded in 1989 under the name Stroitel Sabirabad and later renamed İnşaatçı Sabirabad after the independence of Azerbaijan in 1992. The club participated in the early seasons of the national league system following independence.

The team competed in the Azerbaijan Top Division in the 1992 and 1993 seasons. In its debut season, it finished 12th, and in 1993 it placed 8th in its group before being relegated under league restructuring rules. Despite its modest league performance, the club had a notable cup run in 1993, reaching the final of the Azerbaijan Cup.

Financial and organizational difficulties led to the club’s dissolution in 1995. Although its existence was relatively short, İnşaatçı Sabirabad is remembered as one of the early participants in the formation of Azerbaijan’s post-independence football system.

name: İmişli FK
content:
İmişli FK is a professional football club based in İmişli, Azerbaijan, currently competing in the Azerbaijan Premier League. The club was re-established in 2022 as a phoenix successor of the former MKT Araz FK, a club that previously played in Azerbaijani football before being dissolved in 2018.

The team has its roots in earlier football structures from the region, with the MKT Araz project originally founded in 2004 and going through several restructurings over the years. After its revival as İmişli FK, the club quickly re-entered the professional system, climbing through the First Division and earning promotion to the top flight for the 2025–26 season.

Today, İmişli FK plays its home matches at the Heydar Aliyev Stadium in İmişli and is considered a developing club in Azerbaijani football. While still building its identity at Premier League level, it represents the return of top-tier football to the region and continues the legacy of its predecessor clubs.
stadium: Heydar Aliyev – MKT Araz Stadium, Cap. 8,500
